meaning: adj.间接获得的,旧的,二手的,做旧货生意的adv.间接地,间接听来,用旧货
examples: I only heard about it secondhand.
They could afford a secondhand car, she thought.
Household appliances were bought secondhand and are outdated.
"Clothing is one of our more challenging practices, " says Jason Lawrence, 35, who mostly buys secondhand.
If the firm that made the car has gone bust, it becomes virtually unsellable secondhand.
